Apologize
Could you upgrade my room, please?
Would it be possible to check in early?
You should speak to the manager about it.
I would appreciate it if you could fix the Wi-Fi.
Could you upgrade my room, and would it be possible to add a late checkout?
There seems to be a discrepancy on my bill. Would you mind checking it?
You should speak to the duty manager. They can resolve it quickly.
Could you help me with this?
Would you mind moving my room?
You should ask for the manager.
Good evening. I have a reservation under Ito. I-T-O.
Welcome to the Harborview. Let me find it. Yes, Ms. Ito, a standard room on the ground floor, two nights.
Thank you, but there seems to be a discrepancy. I booked a superior room with an ocean view. Would it be possible to check?
You are absolutely right, and I apologize for the confusion. Let me fix that for you now.
I appreciate it. And could you confirm the checkout time? My confirmation says eleven, but the card here says ten.
Good catch. Checkout is at eleven, as your confirmation says. I will correct the card. Sorry for the inconvenience.
No problem. One more thing, the Wi-Fi code on the card is not working. Could someone help me resolve it?
Of course. I will reset it and bring you a new code myself. And as a courtesy for the mix-up, I would like to add a complimentary breakfast.
That is very kind. That sounds reasonable. Thank you for sorting it all out so quickly.
It is my pleasure, Ms. Ito. If anything else comes up, just ask for me and I will resolve it. Enjoy your stay.
I have a reservation under Ito. Could you check it, please?
There seems to be a discrepancy. I booked a superior room.
Would it be possible to have a late checkout?
There is a charge I do not recognize. Would you mind checking it?
Could I speak with the duty manager, please?

Good evening, and thank you for your patience at the front desk tonight. This is a message for our arriving guests. We are running a little behind on housekeeping, so a few rooms are not ready yet, and we want to be honest with you about that. If your room is not ready when you check in, please come and see us, and we will hold your bags and offer you a drink in the lounge while you wait. That is the least we can do. Now, a quick word about reservations. If you booked a specific room type, a superior room, an ocean view, a quiet floor, please check your confirmation when you arrive, because now and then the system assigns the wrong category. If you notice a discrepancy, tell us right away. We would much rather fix it at check-in than after you have carried your suitcase upstairs. And if anything at all goes wrong during your stay, a code that does not work, a charge you do not recognize, a checkout time that looks off, do not sit and worry about it. Come to the desk, ask for me by name, and we will resolve it. A good hotel is not one where nothing ever goes wrong. It is one where every problem gets fixed quickly, and with a smile.

Hello, this is a message for Ms. Ito in room fourteen-oh-two. This is Sarah, the duty manager, and I am calling to follow up on the problem you reported at the front desk this afternoon. First, I want to apologize for the inconvenience. You booked a superior room with an ocean view, and we gave you a standard room on the ground floor, and that is our mistake, not yours. So here is what we have done. We have moved you to a superior room on the ninth floor, which does have the ocean view you asked for, and your key cards are ready at the desk whenever you are. As a courtesy, and because you were so patient, we have added a complimentary breakfast for both mornings, and we have removed the minibar charge that you did not recognize. That was the discrepancy on your bill, and you were right to point it out. I think that is a reasonable way to make things right, but if you feel it is not enough, please ask for me directly and I will look at it again. Thank you for letting us fix this, Ms. Ito. Enjoy the rest of your stay in New York.
